conditions.label: Control
conditions.description: Baseline destination information without an explicit friction-reduction nudge.

conditions.label: Transport Access Nudge
conditions.description: Adds route, travel-time, frequency, and transfer guidance.

conditions.label: Opening Hours Nudge
conditions.description: Adds availability, seasonal closure, and check-before-visit cues.

conditions.label: Itinerary Fit Nudge
conditions.description: Adds realistic half-day planning, sequencing, and time-cost guidance.

conditions.label: Combined Nudge
conditions.description: Combines transport, availability, and itinerary-fit guidance.

background_questions.label: Confidence using public transit in unfamiliar places

tasks.prompt: You are planning a half-day visit from Fukui Station to Eiheiji Temple. Decide whether this destination fits your itinerary and choose a basic plan.

baseline.title: Eiheiji Temple
body: Eiheiji Temple is a major Zen Buddhist temple in Fukui Prefecture.
The temple grounds include historic buildings, forested paths, and quiet indoor areas.
Visitors usually spend time walking through the main temple complex and surrounding grounds.

transport_access: Transit note: From Fukui Station, take the direct Eiheiji Liner bus (about 30 minutes) or the Echizen Railway to Eiheijiguchi with a local bus transfer. Direct buses run roughly once or twice an hour.
Planning cue: Check the last return bus before you depart — evening departures are sparse — and buy a round-trip ticket so the return is decided in advance.
opening_hours_availability: Availability note: Check current entry hours before departure. Some temple areas may close earlier than general grounds.
Planning cue: Use official site hours and the latest Google Maps hours together before committing to a late-afternoon visit.
itinerary_fit_time_cost: Itinerary cue: Treat this as a half-day stop, not a quick add-on. Leave buffer time for transfers, walking, and return travel.
Planning cue: Pair with nearby or same-corridor stops instead of placing it between distant attractions.

decision_options: Yes, I would include it in a half-day itinerary
Maybe, but only with more information
No, I would skip it for this itinerary

accuracy_question.label: Which planning issue matters most for this task?

tasks.prompt: You are deciding whether to visit the Fukui Prefectural Dinosaur Museum during a short stay. Decide what information you would need before committing.

baseline.title: Fukui Prefectural Dinosaur Museum
body: The Fukui Prefectural Dinosaur Museum is a major museum attraction with exhibits, fossils, and family-oriented displays.
Visitors often combine the museum with other activities in the surrounding area.
The museum can be a destination anchor for visitors interested in science, families, and indoor attractions.

transport_access: Transit note: From Fukui Station, take the Echizen Railway toward Katsuyama (about 55 minutes), then the connecting shuttle bus to the museum. Trains run about twice an hour; the shuttle is timed to selected trains.
Planning cue: Check the timed connection for your return leg before setting out — missing the shuttle can add up to an hour of waiting.
opening_hours_availability: Availability note: Confirm current opening days, timed-entry rules, and ticket availability before travel.
Planning cue: Avoid assuming same-day entry if your schedule is tight or if visiting during a holiday period.
itinerary_fit_time_cost: Itinerary cue: Use the museum as an anchor stop. Build the day around it instead of treating it as a short detour.
Planning cue: Account for travel time, admission flow, exhibit time, and return travel in one plan.

decision_options: I would keep it as optional
I would remove it from the plan

accuracy_question.label: What should a careful visitor verify before committing?

tasks.prompt: You have arrived at Awara-Onsen Station on the Hokuriku Shinkansen. Decide whether to visit the Tojinbo Cliffs before evening and choose a basic plan.

baseline.title: Tojinbo Cliffs
body: Tojinbo is a stretch of basalt sea cliffs on the Sakai coast, designated a national natural monument.
The site offers cliff-top walking paths, sea views, and a nearby shopping street.
Visitors usually spend one to two hours at the cliffs, plus travel time from the station.

transport_access: Transit note: From Awara-Onsen Station, the Tojinbo-bound bus leaves from the station forecourt and takes about 40 minutes. Buses run roughly once an hour — check the departure board on arrival.
Planning cue: Note the last return bus before you board; after early evening the return options are effectively taxi only.
opening_hours_availability: Availability note: The cliffs are open at all hours, but the shopping street and observation tower keep daytime hours and begin closing in the late afternoon.
Planning cue: Check current hours for the tower and sightseeing boats before departure; sea conditions can suspend boat services.
itinerary_fit_time_cost: Itinerary cue: Allow about three hours door-to-door from Awara-Onsen Station, including the bus both ways and time on the cliffs.
Planning cue: Pair Tojinbo with Maruoka Castle or an Awara onsen stop in the same half-day, rather than combining it with destinations on the far side of Fukui.

decision_options: Yes, I would go this afternoon
Maybe, but only with more information
No, I would skip it for this trip

accuracy_question.label: Which planning issue matters most for this task?
options: Bus schedule and last return time
accuracy_question.correct: Bus schedule and last return time

constructs.label: Information Clarity
items: The information was easy to understand.
The page made the visit requirements clear.
I knew what to check before visiting.

constructs.label: Perceived Friction
items: Planning this visit would require extra effort.
I would feel uncertain about making this plan work.

constructs.label: Planning Confidence
items: I feel confident that I could plan this visit.
I understand the practical steps needed for this visit.
I could explain this plan to another traveler.

constructs.label: Visit Intention
items: I would consider visiting this destination.
I would add this place to a Fukui itinerary.
I would recommend this destination to another traveler with similar interests.

constructs.label: Information Trust
items: The page gave enough detail to support a travel decision.
I would trust this page as a starting point for planning.

final_questions.label: What information was most helpful for making your decisions?
What uncertainty, if any, remained after the tasks?
